title: hearth.drift.detected v1
description: Emitted by hearth-api when an internal consistency invariant is observed to have diverged at runtime. Two surfaces emit on this channel today: (1) 'session_count' — the SSE session snapshot and the HTTP /api/sessions cache disagree on how many sessions are visible (the two paths apply ghost/history/sub-agent filters independently and are only kept in sync by hand; divergence means a phone client oscillates between counts); (2) 'kb_scoring' — reserved for a future runtime check that hearth-api's mirrored kb scoring constants no longer match kb's published values. The compile-time drift_canary_* tests in kb_relevance.rs catch scoring drift in CI; this channel surfaces drift that only manifests at runtime. Non-sensitive internal diagnostics — safe for the public bus.

surface.description: Which invariant diverged. 'session_count' = SSE vs HTTP session-list disagreement; 'kb_scoring' = hearth-api kb scoring mirror vs kb authoritative values.

expected.description: The reference / authoritative value (for session_count: the HTTP-cache session count; for kb_scoring: kb's published constant).

observed.description: The divergent value actually produced by the other code path (for session_count: the SSE snapshot count).

delta.description: observed - expected. Optional convenience field; consumers may compute it themselves.

detail.description: Optional human-readable context, e.g. 'sse=12 http=14; 2 history-mode sessions excluded from SSE only'.

ts.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp when the divergence was observed.
